chunks.text: Stereo. H.C. JD A 38. Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, BAHAWALPUR BENCH, BAHAWALPUR. JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 (The State Vs. Hafiz Muhammad Akmal) Criminal Appeal No. 294 of 2021 (Haji Musheer Ahmad and three others Vs. The State and another) J U D G M E N T Date of hearing: 19.12.2022. Appellants by: Syed Zeeshan Haider, Advocate State by: Ch. Asghar Ali Gill, Deputy Prosecutor General. Complainant by: Khawaja Muhammad Aslam, Advocate. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J. –Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz, Haji Musheer Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz (convicts) were tried by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Ahmadpur East in the case instituted upon the private complaint titled “Muhammad Nazik Vs. Haji Musheer Ahmad and four others” (relating to F.I.R. No. 110 of 2020 dated 23.05.2020 registered at Police Station Dhur Kot, District Bahawalpur) in respect of offences under sections 302, 324,148 and 149 P.P.C. for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 25.06.2021, convicted Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz, Haji Musheer Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 2 Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz (convicts) and sentenced them as infra: Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz: Death under section 302(b) P.P.C. as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.200,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased namely Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (deceased); in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment.

The learned trial court vide judgment dated 25.06.2021, convicted Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz, Haji Musheer Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 2 Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz (convicts) and sentenced them as infra: Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz: Death under section 302(b) P.P.C. as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.200,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased namely Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (deceased); in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. Haji Musheer Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz:- i) Simple Imprisonment for ten years under section 324 PPC and directed to pay fine of Rs. 100,000/- and in default of payment of fine, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. ii) Simple Imprisonment for three years under section 337- F(iii) P.P.C. and directed to pay Daman of Rs. 50,000/- to Nazir Mai (PW-2) and in case of failure to pay the amount of Daman, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. All the sentences awarded to the convict were ordered to run concurrently by the learned trial court. Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz:- i) Simple Imprisonment for ten years under section 324 PPC and directed to pay fine of Rs. 100,000/- and in default of payment of fine, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. ii) Simple Imprisonment for three years under section 337- F(iii) P.P.C. and directed to pay Daman of Rs. 50,000/- to Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and in case of failure to pay the amount of Daman, the convict was directed to undergo further six months of simple imprisonment. All the sentences awarded to the convict were ordered to run concurrently by the learned trial court. Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz:- Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 3 Simple Imprisonment for two years under section 337- A(i) P.P.C. and directed to pay Daman of Rs. 10,000/- to Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and in case of failure to pay the amount of Daman, the convict was directed to undergo further one month of simple imprisonment. 2.

Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz:- Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 3 Simple Imprisonment for two years under section 337- A(i) P.P.C. and directed to pay Daman of Rs. 10,000/- to Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and in case of failure to pay the amount of Daman, the convict was directed to undergo further one month of simple imprisonment. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz, Haji Musheer Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz (convicts), lodged the Criminal Appeal No.294 of 2021, assailing their convictions and sentences. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.11 of 2021 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentence of death awarded to the appellant, namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz. We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal No. 294 of 2021 and the Murder Reference No.11 of 2021 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the necessary facts of the prosecution case, as stated by Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), the complainant of the case, are as under:- “Stated that on 23.05.2020 at about 07:00 PM, I alongwith my mother Nazir Mai and Muhammad Asif went for a khairat on motorcycle. When we reached near the house of accused persons near a Madarsa, the 5 persons came there. Those 5 persons were Hafiz Akmal, Muhammad Ajmal, Haji Musheer all armed with 30-bore pistol, Akram armed with Danda and one unknown person armed with Danda. The accused persons asked us that they would teach a lesson to us for quarrel of children. Akmal accused made a straight fire at Asif which landed in his belly. Muhammad Asif fell down on the ground. Ajmal accused made a straight fire which landed on my left leg which went through and through. Akram and unknown person had been inflicting Danda blows on my body. Haji Musheer accused made a straight fire which landed at left hip of my mother Nazir Bibi and went through and through. Then PWs Nadir Hussain and Mulazim Hussain came at the spot who rescued us form the accused persons.

Haji Musheer accused made a straight fire which landed at left hip of my mother Nazir Bibi and went through and through. Then PWs Nadir Hussain and Mulazim Hussain came at the spot who rescued us form the accused persons. We went to BVH Bahawalpur. Deceased Asif succumb to the injuries and died. We came back at R.H.C. Uch-Sharif for post mortem of Muhammad Asif. Iqbal SI came there and recorded my statement and took my thumb impression on a blank paper. Then police went to my mother and my mother told the whole story. After registration of case, when I got the FIR I came to know that I.O. did not recorded the time of occurrence as well as regarding the Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 4 motorcycle. I asked the I.O. about these faults. He asked me not to worry and he would rectify these faults. Later on after investigation I came to know that I.O. of the case affected the recovery of Danda form Muhammad Ajmal who fired at me and he declared Haji Musheer as innocent, who made fire at my mother Nazir Mai. I.O. assured us to rectify the above stated mistake. One Nasir is relative of accused persons is driver of PS Dhorrkot. He managed the I.O. of the case and I.O. of the case has ruined our case. Being dissatisfied of investigation, I moved private complaint EX-PA before the court. Accused persons be punished.” 4. The accused were summoned to face trial in the case instituted upon the private complaint titled “Muhammad Nazik Vs. Haji Musheer Ahmad and four others” (relating to F.I.R. No. 110 of 2010 dated 23.05.2020 registered at Police Station Dhur Kot, District Bahawalpur) in respect of offences under sections 302, 324,148 and 149 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (deceased). The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 05.01.2021, to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5.

The ocular account of the case was furnished by Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3).. 6. Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 5 Muhammad Arif 985/H.C. (CW-4) further stated that on 29.05.2020, he received a sealed parcel said to contain a pistol from the Investigating Officer of the case, which on 09.06.2020, he handed over the said sealed parcel to the Investigating Officer of the case, for its onward transmission to the office of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Jabbir Ali, 690/C (CW-5) stated that on 23.05.2020, he escorted the dead body of the deceased to the hospital and received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Medical Officer after the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased. Jabbir Ali, 690/C (CW-5) further stated that on 29.05.2020, the appellant namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal got recovered pistol (P- 12), which was taken into possession through recovery memo (CW-5/H), whereas on the same day, the appellant namely Muhammad Ajmal got recovered a Sota (P- 6) which was taken into possession through recovery memo (CW-5/J), whereas the appellant namely Muhammad Akram got recovered a Sota (P-7) which was taken into possession through recovery memo (CW-5/K).

Jabbir Ali, 690/C (CW-5) further stated that on 29.05.2020, the appellant namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal got recovered pistol (P- 12), which was taken into possession through recovery memo (CW-5/H), whereas on the same day, the appellant namely Muhammad Ajmal got recovered a Sota (P- 6) which was taken into possession through recovery memo (CW-5/J), whereas the appellant namely Muhammad Akram got recovered a Sota (P-7) which was taken into possession through recovery memo (CW-5/K). Muhammad Rafique, Patwari (CW-6) prepared the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.CW-6/M). Muhammad Iqbal, SI (CW-7), investigated the case from 23.05.2020 till 10.06.2020, arrested the appellants namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz, Haji Musheer Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz, on 27.05.2020 and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. 7. The learned trial court also examined Dr. Muhammad Shahid (CW-1), who on 23.05.2020 was posted as Medical Officer at R.H.C. Uch Sharif and on the same day examined Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (then injured later deceased). Dr. Muhammad Shahid (CW-1), on examining Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain (then injured later deceased), observed as under:- Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Edges of wounds were inverted and margins were burnt. Probe goes into the abdomen. It was the wound of entrance. Injury No.2 A lacerated wound of 01 cm X 01 cm on the left side of abdomen, 07 CM away from umblicus. Edges were averted with irregular margins. It was the wound of exit. Note: Patient was referred to BVH emergency for treatment and final opinion will be given after receiving surgical notes. Possibility of fabrications. No. Probable duration of injuries. Fresh Kind of weapon. Fire arm.” On the same day, Dr. Muhammad Shahid (CW-1) also conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased, namely Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain.

Uch Sharif and on the same day examined the prosecution witness namely Nazir Mai, (PW-2). Injury No.1 A lacerated wound on medial side of right buttock, 1-0.5 half CM, muscle deep. Note: Patient was refer (sic) to BVH emergency for treatment and final opinion will be given after receiving surgical notes. "Regarding surgical notes No.209681-401-50 dated 23.05.2020. Wound wash and wound debridement was done and A.S.D. placed. The patient was admitted in ward and discharge on 26.05.2020 in satisfactory position. I received surgical notes from BVH Bahawalpur on 25.06.2020. Possibility of fabrications. No. Probable duration of injuries. Fresh Kind of weapon. Fire arm.” 8. On 28.05.2021, the learned Assistant District Public Prosecutor closed the prosecution evidence after tendering in evidence, the report of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore regarding the analysis of the blood-stained earth (Exh. P.D.) and the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh. P.E.) regarding the comparison of the empty shells of the bullets and the pistol (P-12) recovered from the appellant namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal. 9. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellants namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz, Haji Musheer Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 9 Akram son of Shah Nawaz under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to the question why this case against you and why the P.W.s have deposed against you, the appellants replied that they had been involved in the case falsely and were innocent. The appellants, namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal son of Shah Nawaz, Haji Musheer Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz, opted not to get themselves examined under section 340 Cr.P.C, and did not adduce any evidence in their defence. 10. At the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Ahmadpur East convicted and sentenced the appellants as referred to above. 11. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellants precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and that the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible and relevant evidence.

At the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Ahmadpur East convicted and sentenced the appellants as referred to above. 11. The contention of the learned counsel for the appellants precisely is that the whole case is fabricated and false and that the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible and relevant evidence. The learned counsel for the appellants in support of this appeal, further contended that the story of the prosecution mentioned in the F.I.R., on the face of it, was highly improbable and the reason assigned by the complainant and the eye- witnesses for being present at the place of occurrence was without any justification. He further contended that the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellants further submitted that the recoveries were full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value and result of fake proceedings. He finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the accused beyond the shadow of doubt. 12. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, contended that the prosecution has proved its Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 10 case beyond the shadow of doubt by producing independent witnesses. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further argued that the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellant namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3). The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, further argued that the recovery of the pistol from the appellant namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal and the report of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.

The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3). The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, further argued that the recovery of the pistol from the appellant namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal and the report of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh. P.E.) also corroborated the ocular account. The learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant further contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offenders with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant, prayed for the rejection of the appeal. 13. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellants, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant and, with their assistance, perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial. 14. The whole prosecution case against the appellants revolves around the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3). The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) were related to each other and the deceased. Their relationship with the deceased is on record. The deceased namely Muhammad Asif son of Khadim Hussain was the Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 11 paternal cousin of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), whereas the prosecution witness namely Nazir Mai, (PW-2) is the mother of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and the prosecution witness namely Nadir Hussain (PW-3), the paternal cousin of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1). It was also brought on record during cross- examination that none of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW- 1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) had any enmity with either of the appellants, which could have motivated them to depose falsely against the appellants.

Appeal No. 294 of 2021 11 paternal cousin of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), whereas the prosecution witness namely Nazir Mai, (PW-2) is the mother of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and the prosecution witness namely Nadir Hussain (PW-3), the paternal cousin of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1). It was also brought on record during cross- examination that none of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW- 1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) had any enmity with either of the appellants, which could have motivated them to depose falsely against the appellants. The prosecution witness namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) during cross-examination, stated as under:- “ Asif deceased had no dispute with accused Muhammad Akmal or any other accused. Similarly I or my mother had no dispute of any kind with any of the accused..” Similarly, the prosecution witness namely Nazir Mai, (PW-2), during cross- examination, stated as under:- “ We had no dispute of any kind with any of accused persons.” We have noted that the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3), in a very natural and forthright manner, narrated the incident and gave each and every detail of the same, necessary to prove the relevant facts. The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and Nazir Mai, (PW-2) explained that they, along with the deceased, had arrived at the place of occurrence on the motorcycle (P-11), when they were attacked by the appellants. The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) also gave the individual roles of the appellants acted out by them during the occurrence and remained consistent with Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 12 regard to the same. The motorcycle (P-11), which had been used by prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and the deceased to arrive at the place of occurrence, was found present at the place of occurrence, at the time of the visit of Muhammad Iqbal, SI (CW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, at the place of occurrence and Muhammad Iqbal, SI (CW-7) took the said motorcycle (P-11) into possession through recovery memo (Exh.CW-7/N).

Appeal No. 294 of 2021 12 regard to the same. The motorcycle (P-11), which had been used by prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and the deceased to arrive at the place of occurrence, was found present at the place of occurrence, at the time of the visit of Muhammad Iqbal, SI (CW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, at the place of occurrence and Muhammad Iqbal, SI (CW-7) took the said motorcycle (P-11) into possession through recovery memo (Exh.CW-7/N). The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) also explained that the injured and the deceased were taken to the hospital immediately. The statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) prove that they were present at the place of occurrence at the time of occurrence and that they had also witnessed the occurrence. The defence could not prove that the said witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) were not present at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence. We have scrutinized the evidence of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) and find that they were proved to be truthful witnesses and implicit reliance can be placed upon their statements as against the appellants. There is no evidence on record that the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) were motivated by any enmity to depose against the appellants. As mentioned above, no animus between the appellants and the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) existed prior to the occurrence, which could have interested prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) to get the appellants involved in this occurrence falsely. The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 13 Nadir Hussain (PW-3) were subjected to cross-examination but the defence failed to make cracks in their deposition and the veracity of their statements has been proved.

The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 13 Nadir Hussain (PW-3) were subjected to cross-examination but the defence failed to make cracks in their deposition and the veracity of their statements has been proved. The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3) by no stretch of the imagination, can be declared as chance witnesses, as pressed time and again by the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the appellants, disregarding the fact that the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and Nazir Mai, (PW-2) were injured, because they have rendered a plausible justification for their presence at the place of occurrence. During the course of investigation, Muhammad Iqbal, Si (CW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, also collected the blood stained earth from the place where the blood of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and the deceased had fallen and took the same into possession through recovery memo (Exh. P.B.). The report of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh. P.D.) establishes that the said blood taken from the place of occurrence was of human origin. The place of occurrence was further established by the rough site plan (Exh. CW-7/P.) as prepared by Muhammad Iqbal, Si (CW-7) and the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh. CW-6/M) as prepared by Muhammad Rafique, Patwari (CW-6). There is no such material available on record which would indicate the substitution of the appellants in the case with the real culprits. Substitution is a phenomenon of a rare occurrence because even the interested witnesses would not normally allow real culprits for the murder of their relatives let off by involving innocent persons. In this context, reference can usefully be made to the case of “Irshad Ahmad and others v.

Substitution is a phenomenon of a rare occurrence because even the interested witnesses would not normally allow real culprits for the murder of their relatives let off by involving innocent persons. In this context, reference can usefully be made to the case of “Irshad Ahmad and others v. The State and others” (PLD 1996 SC 138). The appellants have not been able to establish any animosity of the complainant or the police for their false involvement in the case. The prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1), Nazir Mai, (PW-2) and Nadir Hussain (PW-3), in a Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 14 straightforward manner, held the appellant namely Hafiz Muhammad Akmal responsible for the Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Asif (deceased) and held the appellants namely Haji Musheer Ahmad son of Shah Nawaz, Muhammad Ajmal son of Shah Nawaz and Muhammad Akram son of Shah Nawaz for causing injuries to the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Nazik (PW-1) and Nazir Mai, (PW- 2). Mere relationship of the prosecution witnesses with the deceased and inter-se is not sufficient to discredit their testimony. In this regard, reference is made to the judgment in the case titled as “Ijaz Ahmad v. The State” (2009 SCMR 99) wherein the August Supreme Court of Pakistan was pleased to observe as under:-- "In the wake therefore, it proceeds that merely because the witnesses are kith and kin, their evidence cannot be rejected, if otherwise it is trustworthy. It would also be pertinent to mention here that related witnesses some time, particularly in murder cases, may be found more reliable, because they, on account of their relation-ship with the deceased, would not let go the real culprit or substitute an innocent person for him." 15. A lateral orientation is a position away from the midline of the body.The umbilicus is Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. Appeal No. 294 of 2021 22 the fibrous remnant of the fetal attachment of the umbilical cord after birth. All layers of the anterior abdominal wall fuse at the umbilical ring, a small round defect in the linea alba located just inferior to the midpoint between the xiphoid process of the sternum and the pubic symphysis. The overlying skin is adherent to the umbilicus, causing a depression that is more accentuated with greater body mass. The umbilicus lies along the midline at the level of the intervertebral discs between the third and fourth lumbar vertebrae The umbilicus is composed of four parts/structures: the mamelon, cicatrix, cushion and furrows.The mamelon is the area of the central hump of the umbilicus. The cicatrix forms the dense scar and is a fusion of several peritoneal and fibrous structures (round ligament of liver, median umbilical ligament, two medial umbilical ligaments, transversalis fascia, umbilical fascia and parietal peritoneum). The cushion of the umbilicus is the slightly raised skin margin which surrounds the mamelon and cicatrix. Finally, the furrows forms the depression inside the cushion, surrounding the mamelon. Section 324 of the P.P.C. deals with an attempt to commit Qatl-i-Amd. An attempt as an indictable crime means an intentional act with a view to attaining a definite end but which is not achieved because of a circumstance, independent of the will of the offender, who makes the Murder Reference No. 11 of 2021 Crl. The intention coupled with some overt act to achieve that intention amounts to crime as it is an attempt to commit a crime. An attempt is known as preliminary crime or inchoate crime as it is something which is not yet complete. Inchoate means just begun, incipient, in the early stages. The inchoate crime is observed as a crime committed by doing an act with the purpose of affecting some other offence.
